title: Water Loss and Non-Revenue Water Detection
spl:
index=water sourcetype="water:flow"
| eval nrw_pct=if(supply_m3_day>0, round(100*(supply_m3_day-billed_m3_day)/supply_m3_day,2), null)
| where nrw_pct > 25 OR min_night_flow_m3h > 10
| bin _time span=1d
| stats latest(nrw_pct) as nrw_pct, latest(min_night_flow_m3h) as mnf by zone_id, _time
| sort - nrw_pct
| table _time, zone_id, nrw_pct, mnf
description: Comparing master meter inflows to zone consumption and night minimum flows highlights leaks, unauthorized use, and meter drift—supporting NRW reduction programs.
value: Service and risk leaders use signals from Water Loss and Non-Revenue Water Detection to move crews, budget, and customer or regulator communications while the situation is still controllable, so the organization steers from live data rather than a post-incident report.
implementation: Align daily rollups to billing cycles. Use minimum night flow from 2–4 AM window. Join pipe age and material via GIS for remediation prioritization. **Domain context:** NRW (non-revenue water) programs align with IWA/AWWA water balance methods—interpret `nrw_pct` with unmetered consumption and authorized unbilled use in mind. **Splunk:** `supply_m3_day` vs `billed_m3_day` timing must align; AMI lag can inflate apparent NRW.
visualization: Choropleth or map (NRW % by zone), Time chart (NRW trend), Bar chart (zones over threshold).
knownFalsePositives: NRW noise from unmetered use, main breaks under repair, night-flow studies, or billing-cycle lag the water-audit program treats as a known reconciliation item.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: AMI + district meter HEC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=water` `sourcetype="water:flow"` (zone_id, supply_m3_day, billed_m3_day, min_night_flow_m3h).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Align daily rollups to billing cycles. Use minimum night flow from 2–4 AM window. Join pipe age and material via GIS for remediation prioritization. **Domain context:** NRW (non-revenue water) programs align with IWA/AWWA water balance methods—interpret `nrw_pct` with unmetered consumption and authorized unbilled use in mind. **Splunk:** `supply_m3_day` vs `billed_m3_day` timing must align; AMI lag can inflate apparent NRW.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=water sourcetype="water:flow"
| eval nrw_pct=if(supply_m3_day>0, round(100*(supply_m3_day-billed_m3_day)/supply_m3_day,2), null)
| where nrw_pct > 25 OR min_night_flow_m3h > 10
| bin _time span=1d
| stats latest(nrw_pct) as nrw_pct, latest(min_night_flow_m3h) as mnf by zone_id, _time
| sort - nrw_pct
| table _time, zone_id, nrw_pct, mnf
```

Understanding this SPL

**Water Loss and Non-Revenue Water Detection** — Comparing master meter inflows to zone consumption and night minimum flows highlights leaks, unauthorized use, and meter drift—supporting NRW reduction programs.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=water` `sourcetype="water:flow"` (zone_id, supply_m3_day, billed_m3_day, min_night_flow_m3h). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): AMI + district meter HEC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: water; **sourcetype**: water:flow. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=water, sourcetype="water:flow". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **nrw_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where nrw_pct > 25 OR min_night_flow_m3h > 10` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by zone_id, _time** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Water Loss and Non-Revenue Water Detection**): table _time, zone_id, nrw_pct, mnf


Step 3 — Validate
Compare levels, flows, or pump metrics with SCADA faceplates or the plant’s process historian for the same site IDs. Confirm maintenance windows are tagged so expected shutdowns do not look like incidents.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Choropleth or map (NRW % by zone), Time chart (NRW trend), Bar chart (zones over threshold).
grandmaExplanation: We watch supplied versus billed water. We help you find real loss before non-revenue water explodes on the books.
